questions.stem: This electron micrograph shows a single bacterium with one long, thin whip-like appendage extending from one pole. What is the primary function of that appendage?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Transfer of plasmid DNA between cells

choices.label: D
choices.text: Uptake of nutrients across the membrane

explanation.summary: The polar filament is a flagellum, a protein cylinder anchored in the cell envelope that rotates to propel the bacterium; adhesion is a role of pili/fimbriae, and conjugative transfer uses sex pili.
explanation.whyCorrect: The polar filament is a flagellum, a protein cylinder anchored in the cell envelope that rotates to propel the bacterium; adhesion is a role of pili/fimbriae, and conjugative transfer uses sex pili.

questions.stem: This labeled schematic depicts a cell whose genetic material sits free in the cytoplasm as a single loop rather than inside a membrane-bound compartment. What type of cell is illustrated?

explanation.summary: The absence of a nucleus with the nucleoid/circular DNA free in the cytoplasm, plus a capsule, cell wall, pili and flagellum, identifies this as a prokaryotic bacterial cell.
explanation.whyCorrect: The absence of a nucleus with the nucleoid/circular DNA free in the cytoplasm, plus a capsule, cell wall, pili and flagellum, identifies this as a prokaryotic bacterial cell.

questions.stem: In this Ziehl-Neelsen (acid-fast) preparation, the background and most cells stain blue while a few slender, curved organisms remain bright red. What cell-wall component allows those red organisms to retain the carbol fuchsin after acid-alcohol washing?

explanation.summary: Acid-fast organisms such as Mycobacteria have waxy mycolic acids in their walls that resist decolorization by acid-alcohol, so they hold the red carbol fuchsin while other cells take up the methylene blue counterstain.
explanation.whyCorrect: Acid-fast organisms such as Mycobacteria have waxy mycolic acids in their walls that resist decolorization by acid-alcohol, so they hold the red carbol fuchsin while other cells take up the methylene blue counterstain.

questions.stem: This smear shows paired, kidney-bean-shaped diplococci that stain pink/red and lie within a host neutrophil. Why do these organisms take the pink color rather than staining purple?

choices.label: A
choices.text: They are coated in waxy mycolic acids

choices.label: B
choices.text: Their wall is rich in teichoic acids

choices.label: C
choices.text: They possess only a thick polysaccharide capsule

choices.label: D
choices.text: They have a thick peptidoglycan cell wall

choices.label: E
choices.text: Their envelope has only a thin peptidoglycan layer beneath an outer lipopolysaccharide membrane

explanation.summary: Gram-negative diplococci lose the crystal violet during decolorization because their thin peptidoglycan sits between inner and outer membranes, so they take up the pink safranin counterstain.
explanation.whyCorrect: Gram-negative diplococci lose the crystal violet during decolorization because their thin peptidoglycan sits between inner and outer membranes, so they take up the pink safranin counterstain.
